Task: Install Flutter SDK only if your project is Flutter
Why / Notes: Skip if this is a native Android-only project

Task: Run flutter doctor only if Flutter is installed
Why / Notes: Checks Flutter/Dart/Android integration

Task: Install Android NDK only if the project or a plugin asks for it
Why / Notes: Not needed for many apps

Task: Install libglu1-mesa only if a tool later complains
Why / Notes: Likely unnecessary if you skip the emulator

Task: Optionally install openssh-server if you want host VS Code to edit files in the VM
Why / Notes: Useful for Remote SSH workflow

Task: Clone or copy your mobile project into the VM
Why / Notes: Gets your code into the dev environment

Task: Open the project in Android Studio or VS Code
Why / Notes: Start actual development work
Confirms toolchain is working

Task: Prefer testing on a real Android phone instead of an emulator inside the VM
Why / Notes: Avoids heavier RAM use and nested virtualization pain

Task: Do not recreate Docker-only environment variables like DEBIAN_FRONTEND, ANDROID_HOME, FLUTTER_HOME unless you want custom paths
Why / Notes: Those were mainly for the container image

Task: Do not create the Docker 'developer' user or /app container workspace
Why / Notes: In the VM you already have a normal Ubuntu user account

Task: Do not manually unpack Android command-line tools under /usr/local unless you intentionally want a CLI-only setup
Why / Notes: Android Studio handles SDK setup more simply in a desktop VM
